metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_1612
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Osborne et al perform subcellular fractionation of rat brains and show co-distribution of syt-1 and syt-2 in fractions eluted from a size-exclusion column that are highly enriched in synaptic vesicles (Fig. 1). The presence of syt-1 and syt-2 in these fractions is monitored by immunoblotting using isoform specific antibodies (antigenic sequences in the N-terminal, where sequence similarity between syt-1 and syt-2 is low). The authors suggest that both syts are localized to the same population of SVs and verify their observation by immunoisolation SVs with anti-syt-1 IgGs and WB for SV marker protein synaptobrevin-2 and syt-2. Additionally, Osborne et al solubilize proteins from crude SV fractions from rat cortex and cerebellum with octylglucoside and perform immunoprecipitation with anti-syt-1 antibodies and observe specific enrichment of syt-2 in the IP sample in contrast to e.g. syt-4, suggesting that syt-1 and syt-2 form a hetero-oligomeric complex. Interestingly, stronger immunoblotting signals for syt-2 are detected in the cerebellum in line with observations by others (see Geppert et al 1991 in additional literature).

22/11/2017 Pim
- Based on the known transmembrane topology of the protein, the "integral component of ... membrane" term was chosen.. Experimental description: Materials and Methods:
Synaptic Vesicle Purification and Immunoisolation-SSV from rat
brain cortex were prepared following the method of Huttner et al. (49)
with minor variations.
